articles.title: Musk bets Tesla's AI future on Intel node that isn't finished yet
articles.snippet: EV maker leaning on still-in-development 14A process for Terafab, says it needs to build own silicon Elon Musk used Tesla's latest earnings call to reveal plans to build AI chips on Intel's not-yet-finished 14A process – a bet on silicon that doesn't exist.…
articles.ai_summary: Elon Musk announced Tesla's plans to develop AI chips using Intel's unfinished 14A process during an earnings call, highlighting a significant reliance on unproven technology. This move underscores Tesla's ambition to create its own silicon for AI applications.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights Tesla's aggressive push into AI hardware despite risks.

articles.title: Will fusion power get cheap? Don’t count on it.

Lithium-ion batteries are now about 90% cheaper than they were in…
